articles.title: Democrat rips Polymarket for taking bets on fate of US pilot shot down over Iran
articles.summary: A Democratic politician is criticizing Polymarket for allowing users to bet on whether a US pilot shot down over Iran will survive or be rescued. The controversy highlights how prediction markets can face political backlash when they create markets on sensitive geopolitical events involving human lives.

articles.title: Can anything stop Kalshi?
articles.summary: Kalshi has become the dominant force in US prediction markets, steamrolling competitors and regulatory hurdles with aggressive expansion and deep pockets. Their growing monopoly could either legitimize prediction markets for mainstream adoption or create dangerous market concentration that stifles innovation and fair pricing.

articles.title: Claude AI powered trading bot turns $1 into $3.3 million on Polymarket
articles.summary: A trading bot powered by Claude AI reportedly transformed a $1 investment into $3.3 million on Polymarket, delivering a 3,300,000% return that would make even the most degenerate gamblers weep with envy. This marks another milestone in AI-powered prediction market trading, suggesting that sophisticated algorithms are starting to identify and exploit pricing inefficiencies faster than human traders can blink.

articles.title: Prediction markets: the hunt for the new ‘dumb money’
articles.summary: Prediction markets are actively seeking out less sophisticated traders to provide liquidity and betting opportunities for sharper players. This hunt creates better odds for informed bettors while potentially expanding market participation, though it raises questions about whether these platforms are becoming more like casinos than information aggregation tools.

articles.title: Prediction markets challenge tribal casinos’ hard-won place in US gambling
articles.summary: Tribal casinos are pushing back against the expansion of prediction markets, viewing them as a threat to their exclusive gambling privileges that took decades to secure. If tribes successfully lobby to restrict prediction markets, it could kill the industry's momentum just as it's gaining mainstream traction with platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket.

articles.title: First Prediction Market Note Offers Nvidia Bets With Less Risk
articles.summary: A new financial instrument lets investors bet on Nvidia's performance through prediction markets while reducing their downside exposure. This could attract risk-averse traders who want tech exposure without the full volatility, potentially bringing more mainstream money into prediction markets.

articles.title: US sues Arizona, Connecticut, Illinois to stop regulation of prediction markets
articles.summary: The federal government is suing Illinois to block the state's attempt to regulate prediction markets, likely arguing that betting on political outcomes falls under federal jurisdiction. This legal clash could set a major precedent for whether states can independently crack down on political prediction markets or if Washington gets to call the shots nationwide.

articles.title: Polymarket taps Pyth Network to resolve new US equity and commodity markets
articles.summary: Polymarket is partnering with Pyth Network's price feeds to automatically settle prediction markets on US stocks and commodities. This move signals Polymarket's push beyond politics into traditional financial betting, where instant settlement could attract traders tired of waiting for manual resolution.

articles.title: The Problem with Binding News and Prediction Markets
articles.summary: News outlets are increasingly partnering with prediction markets or launching their own betting platforms, creating potential conflicts between journalistic integrity and profit motives. When reporters have financial incentives tied to market outcomes, it corrupts the very information flow that makes prediction markets valuable in the first place.

articles.title: Fake or real, the “inside traders” on Polymarket are great engagement bait
articles.summary: Polymarket traders claiming insider knowledge are driving massive engagement whether their tips are legitimate or pure fiction. These self-proclaimed insiders create viral moments that pull new users into prediction markets, even when their track records crumble under scrutiny.

articles.title: ‘Death Markets’ and Attack Ads: Prediction Rivalry Turns Nasty
articles.summary: Prediction market platforms are trading barbs in increasingly aggressive marketing campaigns, with competitors attacking each other's credibility and labeling rivals as "death markets" that profit from human suffering. The mudslinging signals the industry is maturing beyond its wonky academic origins into a real business where platforms fight dirty for market share and user dollars.

articles.title: Update on Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Prediction markets are seeing significant movement across major platforms as new regulations, platform features, or high-profile events reshape how people bet on future outcomes. These changes will directly impact where bettors can place money, what markets are available, and how much liquidity flows through the ecosystem.
